description:
Jira: TBD (inherits from parent epic mutagen-wrd)

Advances: mutagen.coverage

Deliverable:
- lib/mutagen_ex/coverage_runner.ex L101-106: replace the existing `{:error, :cover_already_running, %{message: ...}}` rejection message with the enriched message:
  ":cover_server is already registered. MutagenEx.TaskSup is the documented singleton owner of :cover_server and ExUnit.Server during a MutagenEx mutation cycle; a competing session is refused to prevent state corruption. See .spec/decisions/supervision_tree.md."
- The check itself stays as it is at L155-160 — single `Process.whereis(:cover_server)` lookup. NO new helper function (no `ex_unit_in_progress?/0`).
- Extend one existing rejection-path assertion in test/mutagen_ex/coverage_runner_test.exs (around L99-130) to additionally assert `assert msg =~ "MutagenEx.TaskSup"`. Use the existing test's setup pattern (sentinel pid registered as `:cover_server`).

Verification:
- Tests to pass:
  - v1 of mutagen.coverage (mix test test/mutagen_ex/coverage_runner_test.exs) green, including the extended rejection-message assertion
  - mix compile --warnings-as-errors green
- Behaviors to demonstrate:
  - r1 (revised) holds: the rejection tuple's message names `MutagenEx.TaskSup`
  - r8 partially holds (the rejection mechanism — the doctrine prose lives in the decision file; the unguarded coverage-phase window is honestly named as a Won't-Have in the decision file's §Won't-Have)

Out of Scope (files):
- lib/mutagen_ex/application.ex (that is S1)
- lib/mutagen_ex/mutation_runner/mutation_loop.ex (that is S2)
- test/mutagen_ex/supervision_test.exs (that is S4)
- README.md, CHANGELOG.md (that is S5)
- everything else under lib/mutagen_ex/

Allowed touches:
- lib/mutagen_ex/coverage_runner.ex (only the message at L101-106)
- test/mutagen_ex/coverage_runner_test.exs (extend one existing assertion only)

Out of Scope (intent):
- Do not add an `ex_unit_in_progress?/0` helper. The architecture's invented second in-flight signal was cut per refined plan SA-1.
- Do not change the rejection-path control flow (the `cover_already_running?/1` check still returns boolean; the caller still pattern-matches it).
- Do not claim atomic exclusion in the message — the multi-second race window during the coverage phase is acknowledged in r8 and supervision_tree.md.

Merge gate:
- [ ] mix compile --warnings-as-errors green
- [ ] mix test test/mutagen_ex/coverage_runner_test.exs green
- [ ] New assertion exercises the enriched message (msg =~ "MutagenEx.TaskSup")
- [ ] Scope check: diff touches only Allowed globs (mechanical)
- [ ] Auditor APPROVE comment on the ticket

comments.text:
IMPLEMENTED
Session: 81a13509-99b2-41c4-9907-6ad88ad2ede0
Log: ~/.claude-personal/projects/-Users-mz-dev-mz-mutagen-ex-mutagen-wrd/81a13509-99b2-41c4-9907-6ad88ad2ede0.jsonl

Commit: e62c337
Files touched:
- lib/mutagen_ex/coverage_runner.ex (rejection message at L101-110 — was 6 lines, now 10 lines after the multi-line concat)
- test/mutagen_ex/coverage_runner_test.exs (added one `assert msg =~ "MutagenEx.TaskSup"` line in the r1 rejection test, plus a two-line comment annotating it)

Tests added: none — extended the existing r1 rejection-path test in coverage_runner_test.exs ("r1: refuses if :cover_server already registered (s1) — returns :cover_already_running and does NOT call configure/run/compile") with one new assertion exercising the enriched message.

Verification stubs flipped: none — `mutagen.coverage.v1` was already `execute: true` on the epic branch (it had already been flipped by 18.1's foundation work that introduced the spec). `mutagen.coverage.v5` (`mix test test/mutagen_ex/supervision_test.exs`, covers r1+r8) remains `execute: false` because the supervision_test.exs file is S4's deliverable (mutagen-wrd.18.4), not S3's.

Summary: Replaced the existing `:cover_already_running` rejection message at coverage_runner.ex L101-106 with the enriched form that names `MutagenEx.TaskSup` as the documented singleton owner of `:cover_server` and `ExUnit.Server` during a MutagenEx mutation cycle and cites `.spec/decisions/supervision_tree.md`. Extended the existing rejection-path assertion in coverage_runner_test.exs with `assert msg =~ "MutagenEx.TaskSup"`. The Process.whereis(:cover_server) check itself is unchanged — single lookup at L155-160, no new ex_unit_in_progress?/0 helper (cut per SA-1). The honest multi-second window during the coverage phase between the check and `:cover.start/0` is acknowledged as a Won't-Have in the decision file and intentionally not closed here. `mix compile --warnings-as-errors` green; `mix test test/mutagen_ex/coverage_runner_test.exs` green (12 tests, 0 failures); full `mix test` green (357 tests, 0 failures).

title: S3: coverage_runner.ex singleton-ownership message enrichment
